zh.lifeSummary: 當Geoffrey de Neville於1225年出生在英國英格蘭達勒姆拉比 卡素時，他的父親Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Neville28歲，母親Joan Monmouth21歲。他和Margaret Longvillers至少有4個兒子。他去世於1285年3月25日在英國藍卡爾宏比 卡素享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

variants.name: Nevells
meanings: Irish: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevils
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Novel
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Neville was born in 1225, in Raby Castle, Durham, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Neville, was 28 and his mother, Joan Monmouth, was 21. He had at least 4 sons with Margaret Longvillers. He died on 25 March 1285, in Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Neville est né en 1225, à Raby Castle, Durham, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Neville, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan Monmouth, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils avec Margaret Longvillers. Il est décédé le 25 mars 1285, à Hornby Castle, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Neville 1225 in Raby Castle, Durham, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Neville 28 und seine Mutter Joan Monmouth 21. Er hatte mit Margaret Longvillers mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 25. März 1285 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Nevilleが1225年にイギリスイギリスダルハムラビー カストルで生まれた時，父Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Nevilleは28歳，母Joan Monmouthは21歳であった。彼はMargaret Longvillersとの間に少なくとも4人の息子をもうけた。彼は1285年3月25日にイギリスランカシュアイアホールンビー カストルで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Neville(이)가 1225년 영국 잉글랜드 뒤람 라비 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Neville(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Monmouth(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Margaret Longvillers(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1285년 3월 25일 영국 랭카실르 호른비 카스틀에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Neville родился в 1225 г. в местности раби кастл, дурам, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Geoffrey de Neville 1st Baron de Neville было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Monmouth, 21. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына от Margaret Longvillers. Он умер 25 марта 1285 г. в местности хаурнби кастл, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.
